# Polaris Documentation

> User and developer documentation for Polaris Finance — the self-scaling stablecoin operating system. Mint pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.

## Overview

- [Polaris Finance Documentation](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/): Polaris is the pETH-powered yield layer for all of DeFi, minting pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.
- [Changelog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/changelog): A documentation changelog for the Polaris docs, in Keep a Changelog style. This tracks changes to the docs only, not protocol release history.
- [Polaris Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts): A one-page map of the Polaris system, with every key term defined briefly and linked to its full explanation.
- [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status): Current Polaris phase, official app URL, supported network, production status, and what is safe to use today.
- [Public Testnet Quickstart](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/quickstart): The fastest safe path to try Polaris on Public Testnet 1 with Sepolia test assets.
- [Troubleshooting](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/troubleshooting): Fix common Polaris app issues on Public Testnet 1.
- [Why Polaris Finance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/why-polaris): The stablecoin trilemma, the degradation pattern, and how Polaris is architected so that trustlessness and scale stop being mutually exclusive.

## Minting & Troves

- [Minting Polaris pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting): How the Polaris CDP model mints pAssets directly from the protocol against pETH collateral, with autonomous market-aware interest rates.
- [Managing a Polaris Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/managing-your-trove): Manage a Polaris trove around its Individual Collateralization Ratio (ICR) — add collateral, mint or repay debt, track interest, and close out cleanly.
- [Open a Polaris Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/open-a-trove): Step-by-step guide to opening a Polaris trove — deposit pETH, mint a pAsset, and pick a safe buffer to stay clear of liquidation and redemption.
- [Polaris Asset Catalog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/passet-catalog): The pAsset debt tokens Polaris can mint (pUSD, pGOLD, pCHF, pBigMac), how pETH differs as collateral, and how StablecoinOS can add new assets.

## pETH & Bonding Curve

- [pETH Bonding Curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth): How one two-way bonding curve gives pETH contract-native liquidity, a rising floor price, and protocol-wide value capture, with no liquidity providers required.
- [pETH Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price): pETH's floor price is a mathematically enforced minimum under the bonding-curve invariants. What it is, why it rises, and how the floor ratio frames downside.
- [pETH Split: fpETH and vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split): How pETH splits into fpETH and vpETH, what each leg represents, how merging works, and how reserve loans use fpETH.

## Earning Yield

- [Polaris Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield): The Stability Pool is a reserve of pAssets that backstops liquidations and is the primary venue for earning yield on Polaris.
- [Polaris Yield Sources](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield/yield-sources): Every source of pAsset yield on Polaris is onchain, internal to the protocol, and scales with activity — yield that is owned, not rented.

## Redemptions & Liquidations

- [pAsset Redemptions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations): A redemption swaps a below-peg pAsset for its pETH collateral at face value minus a fee. How the arbitrage restores peg, and what it means for borrowers.
- [Polaris Liquidations](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations): A trove below its minimum collateralization ratio is liquidated to keep the pAsset backed. The Stability Pool absorbs the debt, or it redistributes to troves.
- [Polaris Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode): Recovery Mode is the protective state Polaris enters when system reserve backing runs thin relative to total debt. How it works, and how to stay safe.

## POLAR & Conversion

- [POLAR Token, Staking, and Conversion](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar): Why POLAR exists, how it is minted only by burning pETH, why its dilution is productive, and how staking and conversion fit together.
- [POLAR Conversion Auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions): The conversion auction bridges pETH and POLAR — a spike-and-decay auction where burning pETH mints POLAR, raises the floor price, and releases ETH yield.
- [POLAR Tokenomics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/tokenomics): How POLAR works: issuance by burning pETH, current staking, future vePOLAR, yield routes, and pending distribution terms.

## Stewardship

- [Polaris Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship): Polaris keeps its core immutable and lets vePOLAR steward only bounded parameters, onboarding decisions, and edge processes that cannot be automated.
- [Fee Router & Flows](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/fee-router): Polaris revenue routing: the Fee Router buffers protocol yield to troves and stakers, while Flows share hard-asset revenue with contracts building on Polaris.
- [vePOLAR Locking and Stewardship](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/vepolar): vePOLAR is vote-escrowed POLAR. Lock POLAR to receive stewardship rights and a share of protocol yield without touching the immutable core.

## Resources

- [Polaris Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources): Every Polaris term defined in one or two sentences, alphabetized and linked to its full explanation. The fast way to look anything up.
- [Polaris Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security): Polaris security model, audit-report status, bug-bounty status, and disclosure guidance. Pre-launch reports and bounty details are not published yet.
- [Polaris Brand Assets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/brand-assets): Polaris logos, the Celestial Night Sky palette, and typography, with usage do's and don'ts. Dark mode is the canonical surface.
- [Polaris Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts): Canonical Polaris contract addresses, deployment shape, and verification rules for production and Public Testnet 1.
- [Polaris Finance FAQ](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/faq): Short answers to common Polaris questions, with links to canonical pages for status, contracts, parameters, risks, and app actions.
- [Polaris Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/parameters): Canonical Public Testnet 1 parameter values, source, network, and production-finality status.
- [Polaris Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure): A comprehensive catalog of the risks of using Polaris, each paired with the mechanism that mitigates it, without implying any risk is eliminated.
- [Polaris Safety & Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification): A pre-signing checklist for verifying the Polaris frontend, network, contracts, parameters, risks, and audit status.
- [Polaris Public Testnet 1 Artifacts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet): Sepolia chain ID, official testnet app, testnet caveats, active pAssets, and manifest locations for Public Testnet 1.

## using-app

- [Dashboard & Analytics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app): How to read the Polaris app Dashboard and Analytics before choosing an action.
- [Advanced](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced): How to use Polaris Advanced tools for approvals, PSM actions, reserve loans, staking, conversion, and manual checks.
- [Borrow](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow): How to open Polaris borrow positions from the app Borrow tab.
- [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn): How to deposit, withdraw, claim, and check Polaris Earn positions.
- [Manage Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/manage-trove): How to add collateral, mint more, repay debt, close, and monitor a Polaris trove.
- [Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split): How to split pETH into fpETH and vpETH, merge them back, and avoid common execution mistakes.
- [Swap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/swap): How to use Polaris Swap with quote, approval, slippage, and route checks.
- [Zap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/zap): How to use Polaris Zap routes and recover safely when one bundled step fails.
